Abstract

The purpose of this project was to build a mark sheet for students (a "student marksheet system"). This is a web-based application, which has made it possible to modernize the administrative and retrieval processes of academic performance records safely. In addition to being a full stack application with a solid architecture that supports a utility first way of thinking about the front end of the application (HTML / TypeScript / JavaScript / TailWind CSS), the system utilizes Supabase (PostgreSQL) as a means of providing a number of reliable and scalable options for data storage.

One of the main features of the system are the two, completely secure and private interfaces designed specifically for students and teachers, as seen in the examples of login and dashboard views. These allow for the protection of private student data, and the right level of access and authority to be given to teachers. The teacher dashboard has full control over entering data, allowing teachers to enter individual marks or upload multiple marks from an excel sheet at once. This makes it easier to manage student data. The system will automatically calculate the students' grades, based on the total percentage earned by the student across all classes: Maths, DBMS, OS, DAA, and Network. That’s removes the load of manually calculating grades, and the cause of error due to manual calculations. Through their own private dashboards, students can access to their own private information, can see marks for each class, and a summary of their performance including their total marks and average percentage. This gives the students with greater transparency of how they are performing academically.

This project greatly improved upon the existing state of affairs by replacing old, disorganized, or manual ways of keeping student records, with a central, up-to-date, secure digital system. This system greatly enhanced the ability of educators to efficiently manage data and to calculate grades, and it allowed students to easily and securely see their own academic performance data. Overall, this system creates a reliable and transparent environment for the management of academic performance.

I extend my special thanks to **Dr. R. R. Singh**, **Head, Centre for Artificial Intelligence**, for his insightful suggestions and constructive feedback which greatly enriched the outcome of my word.
